title: Forestry Corporation of NSW — Environmental impact
investigation_objective: The NSW Environment Protection Authority issued Forestry Corporation of NSW two penalty notices totalling $60,000 (issued 20 January 2026) after inspections found a machinery crossing on Dooga Creek in the Mogo State Forest, a designated Environmentally Significant Area, was not built to best-practice standard, causing sediment erosion and water pollution. The EPA also found Forestry Corporation had failed to complete required clean-up work under a prior clean-up notice.
scope: Issued two NSW EPA penalty notices totalling $60,000 over water pollution from a forestry operation; Alleged to have failed to complete required remediation work under a prior EPA clean-up notice; Incident occurred within a designated Environmentally Significant Area
